identity.name: Chris J Shanku
identity.oneliner: I spent 8 years at Microsoft selling AI. Now I build it - for the life I almost lost.

ventures.name: The Grove
ventures.body: The Grove is the personal life operating system I built for my family and me - a multi-agent harness that captures what matters across work, recovery, co-parenting, and health, then pushes the right nudge at the right moment. It runs on a homelab I own end to end, and it treats my own life like a product I ship to every day.

ventures.name: Human in the Loop
ventures.body: Human in the Loop is a weekly LinkedIn Live show about building with AI - made with AI at every step, from research to the on-air graphics. Each episode works a real problem in the open instead of pitching a finished answer, so the audience sees the messy middle where a human still has to make the call.

ventures.name: iLink Tools
ventures.tag: AI tools for an FSI sales team
ventures.body: iLink Tools is the internal suite I built with agents so an FSI sales team sells smarter - meeting prep, competitive intel, account research, and regulated-workflow patterns, all wired to the signals a seller actually acts on. Sixteen tools have shipped so far, each one earning its place by removing a real piece of manual work from the week.

exemplars.name: Frontier Forge
exemplars.label: forge.ilink.tools
exemplars.desc: AI prototype builder: describe an agent, get a working prototype.

exemplars.name: Fieldline
exemplars.label: fieldline.ilink.tools
exemplars.desc: FSI intelligence hub: daily Microsoft signals and account intel for FSI sellers.

exemplars.name: WireDesk
exemplars.label: wiredesk.ilink.tools
exemplars.desc: Agent patterns for regulated banking workflows: signature match, PoA review, branch close.

ventures.name: twelfth.help
ventures.body: twelfth.help carries a daily recovery message to the person who needs it, the way the twelfth step asks us to carry it to the next one still struggling. It is a productized sibling of the step work I do every morning - free and open for anyone in recovery, with a managed track for the fellowships and programs that want it run for them.

ventures.name: WayFact
ventures.body: WayFact is an FSI data-curation engine that turns raw market signal into verified, decision-ready fact - the productized graduation of the SignalScope work I built inside iLink Tools. It runs as a standalone venture under TheGrove AI LLC, focused on the one thing regulated financial-services teams cannot outsource to a hallucinating model: knowing what is actually true.

moth.name: The Moth
moth.blurb: Award-winning storyteller and regular performer at The Moth. Stories draw on fatherhood, recovery, career pivots, and the courage to say yes before you know how.

storytelling.mantra: I am curious and compassionate. I am mindful and grateful. I answer the mail and help the next one in line. I live for community and am worthy of success. I am a man of integrity.

rows.role: Senior Director, US Financial Services Practice
bullets: Building a $4M annual revenue pipeline through consultative pre-sales, Microsoft co-sell motions, and direct enterprise engagement across banking, insurance, and capital markets.

Designed and deployed AI-powered operational tools for the practice: automated meeting prep, a voice-preserving email drafting agent with a review gate, and a follow-up engine that risk-scores every commitment.
Built Intel Brief (FSI competitive intelligence) and The Ledger (relationship health across 100+ contacts).
Leading iLink's presence at Microsoft FabCon: workshop proposals, partner engagement, and executive scheduling.
Delivered 97% Azure Data Services revenue in FY25 (97% FY24, 89% FY23).
Led Banking in the FY24 Azure Arc-enablement Hero Motion, enabling >$1.4M in net-new ADS; drove a $1M Databricks win at a Midwest bank.
Facilitated the Ally GenAI case study - the first GenAI use case across all of US FSI - plus Xome Data Modernization, both on Microsoft's Customer Stories.
Named to Microsoft's Executive Briefing Center (EBC) speaker list for AI and Fabric; keynoted the Microsoft + OpenAI partnership to a top-200 exec audience.
Only FSI representative selected for the Atlanta Innovation Hub Ambassadors program, giving tours and demos as a proxy Innovation Hub Architect.

rows.role: Senior Technical Specialist, Intelligence / Power Platform / Power BI (FSI)
bullets: The go-to Power BI expert across all of Financial Services; met or exceeded quota FY18-FY22 (96% VTT with 50% YoY growth in FY21).
Hosted 40+ Power BI Dashboard-in-a-Day workshops; selected to speak at Power BI World Tour (Oct 2018).
Delivered >1,000% VTT in FY20 as Power Platform Technical Specialist; led biweekly PBI SWAT sessions across Modern Work, Data & AI, and Biz Apps.
Led the Modern Data Experience team (12 consultants, $3M+ annual revenue) across business strategy, P&L, and all technical pre-sales; grew the practice by over 500%.
Created ConsulTrain, an internal readiness program that upskilled consultants on customer engagement and consulting excellence.
Hand-picked by Microsoft TSPs to deliver technical pre-sales on Power BI, Power Apps, Flow, Cortana Intelligence, and SQL 2016; sold $1M+ to 15+ EPG and SMS&P clients.
Lead Data & Analytics consultant for self-service BI delivery and technical pre-sales; sold and delivered $150K+ of D&A revenue.
Built Tabular SSAS cubes and Power BI dashboards with complex DAX; established company-wide credibility as the go-to BI reference.

rows.role: BI Architect / Offshore Manager / Delivery Lead
bullets: Wore many hats at the intersection of Accenture's consulting discipline and Microsoft's technical innovation, delivering scalable BI on the SQL Server 2012 stack across complex stakeholder environments.
Led offshore delivery teams (2-5 resources); resolved 500+ defects across 5-10 lines of business; trained dozens of new consultants through 30+-participant classroom seminars.
Where I first learned to say "yes" to opportunity, even when it came disguised as uncertainty.

quotes.text: As long as you live, keep learning how to live.
If someone offers you an amazing opportunity but you are not sure you can do it, say yes - then learn how to do it later.

tools_built.roster_rule: 16 = 13 public tools (15 named tools.json manifest entries minus retired LoanLens and iMandate) + iExpense + Ascent + OnePager, with OnePager called out as standalone by editorial decision even though it ships inside FSI Showcase. This is Chris's hand-curated editorial roster. It is NOT derivable from ilink-tools tools.json (it excludes manifest internal tools ATO Hub and Microsoft FSI Accounts, and adds OnePager which the manifest counts inside the 13). Never recompute this number. Never automate it. Framing is built, never live: two of the 16 are retired and SignalScope is under construction. See TheGrove wiki/decisions/ad192.md.
